mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, the discount-rate channel dominates and equity valuations reprice upward. This predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, especially when inflation is not accelerating at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining for at least 2 consecutive months, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is flat to falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent local threshold signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest after a fresh break higher rather than during already high but stable unemployment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the sign turns positive after repeated UNRATE breakouts.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger end-demand and housing-linked consumption, which supports corporate revenues and lifts equities over the next quarter. This mechanism fails if the sales improvement is accompanied by a rise in unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is neutralized when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and better earnings leverage for cyclicals, which should translate into positive SPX returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when production is improving without a concurrent jump in unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO upswings are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect is absent when UNRATE is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation surprise combined with unchanged policy rates tightens real financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect activates only when CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains near its prior level.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL accelerates for 3 months while FEDFUNDS changes by less than 0.25 percentage points
regime_hint: inflation scare with delayed policy response
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes with flat FEDFUNDS do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in most such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS implies slower payroll growth and weaker aggregate income, which should lower expected profits and weigh on equities over the next week. This is a short-horizon signal because payroll deterioration is quickly discounted by the market.
failure_condition: Reject if week-ahead SPX returns are not negative on average after PAYEMS declines, or if the sign flips positive in repeated payroll contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-led demand slowdown, captured by falling HOUST together with weakening RSAFS, signals broad domestic demand deceleration and should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The hypothesis fails if housing weakness is offset by accelerating retail sales.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls for 2 consecutive months and RSAFS growth slows at the same time
failure_condition: Reject if combined housing and sales weakness does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if returns stay positive when both series deteriorate together.

mechanism_narrative: In a mid-cycle expansion, stable or rising industrial production with low unemployment signals resilient real activity and supports risk assets. The mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month only when production is expanding and unemployment is not rising.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and stable or falling UNRATE do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when PAYEMS is weakening.
